sanskrit:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम्।
सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः॥
transliteration:
utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṃ karma ced aham
saṅkarasya ca kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ
literal_meaning: Would perish / these / worlds / not / I should perform / action / if / I / of confusion / and / maker / I would become / I would ruin / these / beings
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that total withdrawal from action collapses both external society and internal psychic order, producing ‘inner varṇa-saṅkara’—a riot of conflicting impulses that fragment the self and harm others.
psychological_state: Hyper-responsible activation: the ego feels that its slightest inaction could unleash catastrophic inner and outer chaos, reflecting an inflated sense of causal potency.
trigger_scenario: A burnt-out caregiver believes that taking one weekend off will cause family, work, and friendships to implode, so they keep over-functioning until collapse.
anchor_text: If I stop acting, I seed chaos in the world and in myself.
